articles.title: Trump Arrives At G7 Summit Looking For Momentum After Announcing Deal To End Iran War
articles.content:
法国埃维昂莱班（美联社）——美国总统唐纳德·特朗普周一在七国集团（G7）主要工业国家峰会开始访问时宣称，他旨在结束美国与伊朗战争的一项协议是全球安全的潜在突破，也是美国最终翻过与这个长期对手关系新篇章的机会。

这位共和党总统试图明确表示，他抵达埃维昂莱班时带着有利态势与G7领导人进行会谈，其中包括一些此前严厉批评他对这场持续15周冲突处理的领导人。但他没有立即公布这一新兴协议的具体细节。

部分细节仍需在未来60天内通过谈判敲定，特朗普表示谅解备忘录可能在周五签署后公布。

在与峰会东道主法国总统埃马克龙会面后、加入其他领导人工作晚宴之前，特朗普表示：“我认为中东现在将发生很多伟大的事情，非常重要的是，石油价格正在暴跌，股市今天像火箭一样飙升。”

他说：“我们达成的伊朗协议将为世界带来巨大成功。”

协议可能改变G7格局

特朗普与马克龙、英国首相基尔·斯塔默、德国总理弗里德里希·默茨和意大利总理焦尔贾·梅洛尼在未与他们协商就做出参战决定的问题上存在摩擦。但在前往峰会之前达成协议可能会改变为期三天会议的整体氛围。

马克龙在周一会议开始时祝贺特朗普找到了达成协议的途径。

马克龙说：“这对世界和平来说是非常重要的事情。”

特朗普曾反驳这四位欧洲领导人——北约军事联盟成员国——对美国在冲突中缺乏支持的态度。

他预计将与各国领导人讨论霍尔木兹海峡的排雷问题。英国和法国表示，一旦冲突暂停，他们有兴趣协助排雷。担心可能存在水雷是战争期间油轮运输停滞的原因之一，迅速清除水雷对恢复商业船只的信心至关重要。

马克龙周一表示，法国准备在几天内部署资产，包括排雷船只，到该地区提供帮助。

美国高级官员对记者表示，虽然协议规定立即开放海峡并解除美国海军封锁，但要恢复到以前的运输水平还需要数周时间。

马克龙邀请了三个非G7成员国的领导人——埃及、卡塔尔和阿联酋——参加周二关于中东问题的会议，届时伊朗预计将成为核心焦点。

特朗普面临关于对德黑兰经济激励的问题

特朗普曾激烈批评巴拉克·奥巴马总统2015年达成的核协议，称该协议未能阻止德黑兰推进武器研发，并向这个伊斯兰共和国输送了数十亿美元资金。

2018年，特朗普退出了这项名为《联合全面行动计划》的协议。英国、中国、法国、德国、俄罗斯和欧盟也是该协议的签署方。

特朗普在周日接受《纽约时报》采访时，反驳了与奥巴马时代核协议的比较。特朗普说：“我们是从实力的角度进行谈判的。他基本上是在贿赂他们。”

但特朗普没有解释他的协议将如何处理有关伊朗核计划的一些关键问题，包括谁将负责核实伊朗是否遵守协议，以及谁将销毁或移除据信埋在美国去年夏天严重摧毁的核设施地下的972磅（441公斤）高浓缩铀。

美国高级官员周一透露，谅解备忘录包括可能解冻伊朗被冻结的资金、减免制裁以及提供3000亿美元资金帮助重建伊朗——所有这些都将与德黑兰满足某些旨在缓解白宫担忧的基准挂钩。

一些民主党人和强硬派批评者表示，特朗普未能解释他的协议中的经济救济与奥巴马2015年核协议的做法有何不同。

参议院情报委员会民主党领袖参议员马克·华纳周日在哥伦比亚广播公司《面向全国》节目中表示：“尽管他对《联合全面行动计划》有种种批评，但我们当时有国际观察员，实际上有一个联盟，包括欧洲人，俄罗斯和中国都是签署方。”

共和党参议员林赛·格雷厄姆是特朗普的亲密盟友和伊朗强硬派，他表示怀疑，称国会将需要审查并投票表决任何与伊朗的核协议，并表示他希望副总统万斯——“该协议的策划者”——来提交这份协议。

格雷厄姆在社交媒体上表示：“我有些担心，伊朗对该协议的看法似乎与美国谈判团队声称的不同。”

贸易摩擦和乌克兰战争也是焦点

各国领导人预计将讨论一系列经济和贸易问题。特朗普告诉《纽约邮报》，他已警告马克龙，除非巴黎取消对美国科技公司征收的数字税，否则美国“别无选择”只能对法国葡萄酒征收100%的关税，这是他自首届政府以来提出的长期威胁。

目前从欧盟出口到美国的葡萄酒和烈酒面临15%的关税。

马克龙周一在接受法国电视一台采访时表示，“这不是由美国来决定欧洲或法国法律应该是什么样的。”

目前，泽连斯基在法国期间没有安排与特朗普的一对一会谈，但特朗普周日分别与泽连斯基和俄罗斯总统普京进行了电话交谈。

俄罗斯总统助理尤里·乌沙科夫对记者表示，特朗普强调需要结束与乌克兰的敌对行动，并表示他已准备好影响欧洲盟友和基辅朝这一目标努力，包括在G7峰会上。

一位乌克兰官员告诉美联社，泽连斯基曾提议在G7峰会期间与特朗普和欧洲领导人一起会见普京，但克里姆林宫没有回复。这位官员未被授权公开发言并以匿名方式发言。

普京因涉嫌战争罪被国际刑事法院通缉，而法国作为国际刑事法院成员国将有权逮捕他。美国和俄罗斯都反对该法院。

特朗普暗示，在伊朗协议之后，他现在将有更多精力专注于俄乌战争。

他说：“既然这件事已经结束，我们将专注于那件事。”他指的是在2022年2月莫斯科全面入侵其邻国后停止战斗的外交努力。

——

马哈尼从日内瓦报道。美联社记者塞缪尔·佩特雷昆和约翰·莱斯特在巴黎、沃洛德米尔·尤尔丘克在乌克兰基辅、米歇尔·L·普赖斯和科林·宾克利在华盛顿、汤姆·博蒙特在艾奥瓦州得梅因也参与了报道。
articles.summary:
EVIAN-LES-BAINS, France (AP) — President Donald Trump started his visit to the G7 summit of leading industrialized nations on Monday by declaring his agreement aimed at ending the U.S. war with Iran is a potential breakthrough for global security and a chance for the U.S. to finally turn the page in its relationship with longtime adversary Iran.

The Republican U.S. president sought to make clear that he arrived in Evian-les-Bains with the wind at his back for talks with G7 leaders, including some who have been sharply critical of his managing of the 15-week-old conflict, which has led to a surge in global energy prices — though he did not immediately seem willing to lay out the details of what was in the emerging deal.

Advertisement

Some of those details are still to be negotiated in talks over the next 60 days, though Trump said the memorandum of understanding would likely be released after it’s signed on Friday.

“I think a lot of great things are going to happen in the Middle East right now, and very importantly the oil (price) is plummeting down and the stock market is shooting up like a rocket today,” Trump said at a meeting with the summit’s host, French President Emmanuel Macron, before they joined a working dinner with other leaders.

“The Iran deal that we made is going to bring a lot of success to the world,” he said.

Deal could change G7 dynamic

Trump has had friction with Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni over failing to consult them before the decision to go to war. But sealing an agreement before jetting off to the summit might change the dynamic of the three-day gathering.

Advertisement

Macron at the start of Monday’s meeting congratulated Trump for finding a way to an agreement.

“It’s a very important matter for peace of the whole world,” Macron said.

Trump has pushed back on the four European leaders — members of the NATO military alliance — for their lack of support for the U.S. in the conflict.

He’s expected to discuss with leaders the demining of the Strait of Hormuz. Britain and France have expressed interest in assisting with the demining once the conflict is paused. Fear of potential mines is among the reasons tanker traffic has come to a halt during the war, and quickly clearing them will be crucial to regaining the confidence of commercial vessels.

Macron on Monday said France was ready to move within days to deploy assets, including mine-clearing vessels, to the region to help.

Advertisement

Senior U.S. officials told reporters that while the agreement provides for the immediate opening of the strait and lifting of the U.S. naval blockade, it will take weeks for traffic to return to previous levels.

Macron invited the leaders of three nations that aren’t part of the G7 — Egypt, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates — to take part in a session on the Middle East on Tuesday, when Iran is expected to be a central focus.

Trump faces questions about financial incentives for Tehran

Trump had fiercely criticized President Barack Obama for the 2015 nuclear agreement, which Trump argued failed to stop Tehran from advancing toward a weapon and funneled billions of dollars into the Islamic Republic’s coffers.

Advertisement

In 2018, Trump exited the deal, known as the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action. Britain, China, France, Germany, Russia and the European Union were also signatories to the pact.

Trump, in an interview on Sunday with The New York Times, pushed back on comparisons to the Obama-era nuclear deal. “We negotiated from strength,” Trump said. “He was basically paying them off.”

But Trump hasn’t explained how his agreement will address some key issues about Iran’s nuclear program, including who will be in charge of verifying that Iran is complying with the agreement and who will destroy or remove 972 pounds (441 kilograms) of highly enriched uranium believed to be buried under nuclear sites that were badly damaged by U.S. strikes last summer.

Advertisement

The memorandum of understanding includes the possibility of releasing Iran’s frozen funds, sanctions relief and a $300 billion fund to help rebuild Iran — all of which would be tied to Tehran meeting certain benchmarks aimed at assuaging White House concerns, senior U.S. officials told reporters on Monday.

Some Democrats and hawkish critics say Trump has failed to explain how the financial relief in his agreement will differ from what Obama did in the 2015 nuclear deal.

“For all his critique of JCPOA, we had international observers, we actually had an alliance there that included the Europeans, and Russia and China were all signatories,” Sen. Mark Warner, the top Democrat on the Senate Intelligence Committee, told CBS’ “Face the Nation” on Sunday.

Advertisement

Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham, a close ally of Trump and an Iran hawk, expressed skepticism, saying that Congress will need to review and vote on any nuclear deal with Iran, and said he expects Vance — “the architect of the deal” — to present it.

“I am somewhat concerned that Iran’s view of the agreement seems different than what the American negotiating team is claiming,” Graham said on social media.

Trade irritants and the war in Ukraine are also a focus

Leaders are expected to discuss a range of economic and trade issues. Trump told the New York Post he’s warned Macron the U.S. will “have no choice” but to slap 100% tariffs on French wines unless Paris eliminates its digital tax on American tech companies, renewing a long-running threat from him that dates to his first administration.

Advertisement

Wines and spirits exported to the U.S. from the European Union currently face a 15% tariff.

In an interview with broadcaster TF1, Macron said Monday that it’s not “for the United States to decide what European or French law should be.”

Macron also invited Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for a working session with G7 leaders on Tuesday to discuss the Russia-Ukraine war.

At the moment, Zelenskyy is not scheduled to hold one-on-one talks with Trump while they’re both in France, but Trump on Sunday held separate phone calls with Zelenskyy and Russian President Vladimir Putin.

Advertisement

Russian presidential aide Yuri Ushakov told reporters that Trump emphasized the need to end hostilities with Ukraine and stated his readiness to influence European allies and Kyiv toward that goal, including at the G7.

Zelenskyy had offered to meet Putin with Trump and European leaders at the G7 gathering, but the Kremlin didn’t reply, a Ukrainian official familiar with the matter told The Associated Press. The official was not authorized to speak publicly about the matter and spoke on the condition of anonymity.

Putin is wanted by the International Criminal Court for alleged war crimes, and France would be obliged as an ICC member to arrest him. The United States and Russia both oppose the court.

Advertisement

Trump suggested that, after an Iran deal, he’ll now have greater bandwidth to focus on the Russia-Ukraine war.

“Now that this is finished, we’re going to be focusing on that,” he said, referring to diplomatic efforts to stop the fighting that followed Moscow’s full-scale invasion of its neighbor in February 2022.

___

Madhani reported from Geneva, AP writers Samuel Petrequin and John Leicester in Paris; Volodymyr Yurchuk in Kyiv, Ukraine; Michelle L. Price and Collin Binkley in Washington; and Tom Beaumont in Des Moines, Iowa contributed reporting.

Advertisement
Related
president donald trumpG7 summit

articles.title: Stocks Leap Worldwide, And Oil Prices Drop After U.S. And Iran Reach Tentative Deal On War
articles.content:
纽约（美联社）——周一，全球股市强劲反弹，原油价格回落。此前美国与伊朗达成临时协议，延长停火协议并重新开放霍尔木兹海峡，以恢复全球原油流通。

标普500指数上涨1.7%，市场希望此次美伊协议的宣布能够长期解决这场加剧全球通胀的冲突。道琼斯工业平均指数上涨468点，涨幅0.9%，创历史新高。纳斯达克综合指数飙升3.1%。

原油价格下跌后股市获得提振。布伦特原油期货下跌4.8%，至每桶83.17美元，回落至3月初水平。虽然仍高于战前约70美元的价格，但已远低于几周前超过100美元的高点。市场希望油价下跌将减轻家庭和企业的压力，此前受战争影响，从食品到燃料再到化肥的各种商品价格均出现上涨。

伊朗确认了该协议，但并未包含伊朗核计划等问题的最终协议。相关谈判预计将在未来60天内继续进行，这为可能破坏协议的变数留下了空间。即使霍尔木兹海峡按预期于周五全面重新开放，能源行业也可能需要数月时间才能完全恢复。

不过，金融市场已普遍松了一口气。

在华尔街，燃料成本高昂的公司股价立即上涨。联合航空上涨3.9%，邮轮运营商皇家加勒比集团上涨6.6%。

人工智能相关公司的股票也出现上涨。近几周，这些股票剧烈波动，从狂飙突进到屡创新高，再到突然回调。市场担忧这类股票是否因人工智能热潮而涨得太高太快，其剧烈波动有时在数小时内就会逆转方向。

美光科技大涨10.8%，超威半导体上涨7%。英伟达上涨3.5%，是推动标普500指数上涨的最大动力，因为这家人工智能芯片公司是华尔街市值最大的公司，在指数中的权重超过任何其他公司。

埃隆·马斯克旗下的火箭公司SpaceX同时拥有人工智能公司xAI，在华尔街第二个交易日上涨19.6%。其在纳斯达克的成功首秀表明，投资者对人工智能的需求依然旺盛。市场给予SpaceX超过2.1万亿美元的总估值，超过了埃克森美孚、美国银行和可口可乐的市值总和。

在债券市场，美国国债收益率在油价下跌将缓解各国央行加息压力的预期下回落。

10年期美国国债收益率从上周五的4.48%小幅下降至4.47%。

欧洲央行上周因伊朗战争成为全球首个加息的大型央行。高利率可以抑制通胀，但也会拖累经济并压低各类投资品价格，包括股票和加密货币。高利率对估值最高的投资品种冲击尤为明显，一些批评者称人工智能行业存在泡沫，投资膨胀过度。

美联储将于本周晚些时候公布最新利率决定，这将是新任主席凯文·沃什领导下的首次决策。交易员普遍认为美联储在周三结束的两日会议后将维持主要利率不变。

此前交易员不断加大押注，认为美联储今年可能因通胀加速和就业市场强劲而不得不加息。但美伊达成的临时协议使交易员目前预计今年加息的概率仅为57%，低于一周前的71%，据芝加哥商业交易所数据。

华尔街其他方面，流媒体先驱Roku下跌1.9%，此前该公司宣布福克斯公司将以约220亿美元的现金和股票交易收购该公司。

此前媒体报道该交易后，Roku股价上周五已飙升20%。这笔交易将使福克斯获得Roku频道、第一方数据以及超过1亿户全球流媒体家庭用户。福克斯股价下跌16.8%。

总体而言，标普500指数上涨122.83点至7554.29点。道琼斯工业平均指数上涨468.77点至51671.03点。纳斯达克综合指数上涨795.10点至26683.94点。

海外股市方面，亚洲和欧洲指数均出现上涨。日经225指数大涨5%，录得全球最大涨幅之一并收于历史新高。

“这是个好消息，”Monex首席策略师广木孝表示，“外国投资者的买入正在引领市场，他们预期中东局势将缓和。”

韩国综合股价指数涨幅更大，上涨5.2%，部分得益于三星电子等人工智能赢家持续反弹。

伦敦富时100指数表现落后，下跌0.4%。

——美联社商业记者马特·奥托和艾伦·库滕巴赫以及高级制片人小野真裕子对此报道亦有贡献
articles.summary:
NEW YORK (AP) — Stock markets rallied worldwide Monday, and oil prices eased after the United States and Iran reached a tentative deal to extend their ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz to get the global flow of crude going again.

The S&P 500 rose 1.7% on hopes that this time, the announcement of an Iran-U.S. agreement will mean a long-term fix to a conflict that has worsened inflation around the world. The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 468 points, or 0.9%, to a record, and the Nasdaq composite jumped 3.1%.

Advertisement

Stocks got a lift after the price for a barrel of Brent crude oil fell 4.8% to $83.17, back to where it was in early March. While that’s still above its price of roughly $70 from before the war, it’s lower than the $100 plus it cost just a few weeks ago. The hope is that lower oil prices will take pressure off households and businesses, which have had to pay higher prices for everything from food to fuel to fertilizer because of the war with Iran.

Iran confirmed the deal, but it does not include a final agreement on issues like Iran’s nuclear program. Negotiations on that are expected to continue over the next 60 days, which leaves opportunity for hiccups that could derail the agreement. And even if the Strait of Hormuz does fully reopen on Friday as expected, it will likely take months for the energy industry to get back to full speed.

For now, though, relief swept through financial markets worldwide.

On Wall Street, stocks of companies with big fuel bills were instant winners. United Airlines flew 3.9% higher, and cruise operator Royal Caribbean Group rose 6.6%.

Advertisement

Stocks of companies enmeshed in the artificial-intelligence industry also jumped. These stocks have yo-yoed in recent weeks, going from roaring to records to suddenly turning lower. The concern is whether such stocks shot too high, too fast because of AI mania, and their careening moves have sometimes reversed direction by the hour.

Micron Technology rallied 10.8%, and Advanced Micro Devices rose 7%. Nvidia’s climb of 3.5% was the strongest force pushing the S&P 500 upward because the AI chip company is Wall Street’s most valuable company, giving it more weight on the index than any other.

SpaceX, Elon Musk’s rocket company that also owns the AI company xAI, rose 19.6% in its second day of trading on Wall Street. Its successful debut on the Nasdaq suggested plenty of demand still exists among investors for AI. The market has given SpaceX a total value of more than $2.1 trillion, making it bigger than Exxon Mobil, Bank of America and Coca-Cola combined.

Advertisement

In the bond market, Treasury yields eased on hopes that lower oil prices will remove pressure on central banks to raise interest rates.

The yield on the 10-year Treasury slipped to 4.47% from 4.48% late Friday.

Europe’s central bank last week became the first major one in the world to raise interest rates because of the war with Iran. High interest rates can keep a lid on inflation, but they also slow economies and undercut prices for all kinds of investments, including stocks and cryptocurrencies. They hit investments seen as the most expensive in particular, and some critics are calling the AI industry a bubble where investment inflated too far.

The Fed will announce its latest decision on interest rates later this week, which will be the first under its new chair, Kevin Warsh. Traders see it as a near certainty that the Fed will leave its main interest rate steady after its two-day meeting ends Wednesday.

Advertisement

Traders had been raising bets that the Fed may have to raise interest rates this year because of how much inflation has accelerated and how solid the U.S. job market remains. But the tentative deal between the United States and Iran means traders are now betting on only a 57% chance of a hike this year, down from 71% a week ago, according to data from CME Group.

Elsewhere on Wall Street, Roku fell 1.9% after the company announced that Fox Corp. is buying the streaming pioneer in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $22 billion.

Roku’s stock had already soared 20% Friday, when media reports emerged about a deal, which will give Fox access to the Roku channel, first-party data and more than 100 million global streaming households. Fox’s stock fell 16.8%.

Advertisement

All told, the S&P 500 rose 122.83 points to 7,554.29. The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 468.77 to 51,671.03, and the Nasdaq composite jumped 795.10 to 26,683.94.

In stock markets abroad, indexes climbed in Asia and Europe. Japan’s Nikkei 225 leaped 5% for one of the world’s biggest gains and finished at a record.

“This is great news,” said Takashi Hiroki, chief strategist at Monex. “Buying by foreign investors is leading the market with expectations of easing tensions around the situation in the Middle East.”

Advertisement

South Korea’s Kospi soared even more, 5.2%, thanks in part to continued rallies for AI winners like Samsung Electronics.

London’s FTSE 100 was an outlier and slipped 0.4%.

___

AP Business Writers Matt Ott and Elaine Kurtenbach and Senior Producer Mayuko Ono contributed to this report.

Related
united states

articles.title: Scouring The Strait Of Hormuz For Mines Could Take Weeks
articles.content:
伦敦，6月15日（路透社）——航运和海上安全消息人士称，确保霍尔木兹海峡免受水雷威胁可能使该水道重新恢复正常航运的时间推迟数周。

根据五位西方海上安全机构的评估，常规扫雷舰和尖端水下无人机的作业可能需要持续40至50天，才能让大多数保险、航运或石油公司有足够信心通过霍尔木兹海峡。

据基于战前流量数据的估算，这可能使数千万桶石油滞留原地，加上自2月28日美国和以色列攻击伊朗以来海湾地区已被阻断的石油供应。

美国能源信息署上周的分析显示，世界最大经济体的库存正朝着至少自2003年以来的最低水平下降，在此背景下，海湾地区的每一桶出口石油都至关重要。

尽管伊朗和美国近几周悄悄帮助船只通过被封锁的水道，但在美国和伊朗周日表示已达成初步协议、结束战争并重新开放海峡后，航运官员仍敦促保持谨慎。

“目前我们仍认为船只开始通行风险非常高，”航运协会BIMCO首席安全与安保官员雅各布·拉森表示。

“该地区的水雷威胁无论是近期还是更长远来看都是令人担忧的问题，需要建立无雷航道。”

船东寻求保证

目前尚不清楚伊朗可能在海峡布设了多少水雷。该海峡在战争前承担了全球每日石油和液化天然气供应量的20%。

伊朗在战争期间寻求加强对该水道的控制，曾威胁部署海军水雷，但未就其军队是否已布设水雷发表评论。

美国已表示水雷是一个风险，并称已瞄准伊朗布雷船。

6月2日，美国国务卿马尔科·鲁比奥在参议院外交关系委员会听证会上表示，伊朗“在霍尔木兹海峡——国际水域的大片区域布设了水雷”，但未进一步说明。

德国海军在6月11日的一份简报中援引美国和英国海军的情报称，水雷位于海峡周围的四个地点，并补充说德国无法核实这些水雷位置。

即使存在水雷的可能性也可能令企业望而却步。航运业官员表示，一艘超级油轮及其原油货物价值约3亿美元，因此战争风险承保人、石油公司和油轮公司需要获得安全通行的保证后才会尝试通过海峡。

“只需一枚水雷就足以造成人员伤亡，”全球顶级技术船舶和船员管理专业公司之一V.Group的首席执行官雷内·科夫德-奥尔森表示。该公司有13艘船舶被困在海湾地区。

“这显然对全球航运是一个重大问题，”他说。

航运流量仍然低迷

当被问及上周已布设了多少水雷及其位置时，美国军方中央司令部发言人表示，出于行动安全考虑，无法公开讨论具体细节。

“美国军方正在持续努力，确保霍尔木兹海峡完全清除伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队布设的水雷，”该发言人说。

白宫未回应置评请求。

阿曼海上安全中心在5月30日警告在该海峡阿曼一侧航行的船员保持谨慎，此前该中心称发现了“疑似漂浮水雷的物体”。

阿曼信息部未立即回应置评请求。

在美国和伊朗就临时停火协议进行谈判期间，双方都使部分船只得以离开海峡。

美国总统唐纳德·特朗普上周表示，美国一直在运出数百万桶石油。路透社5月曾报道，一些国家已与德黑兰达成协议，确保船只通行。

根据航运数据，通过海峡的船只数量近几周增至每天平均12至15艘。但这些数据只有在船只离开海峡后变得可见时才能得到核实。这仅是战前每天通过该水道120至140艘船只的一小部分。

水雷威胁

3月，在美国和伊朗达成脆弱停火协议之前，伊朗国家安全委员会据伊朗半官方的法尔斯通讯社报道称，任何“敌人”攻击伊朗海岸或岛屿的企图都将导致在整个海湾地区布设航道和通信线路的水雷。

报道称，措施将包括各种类型的海军水雷，包括可从岸上发射的漂浮水雷。

伊朗外交部未回应置评请求。

英国、法国和德国都已向中东派遣军舰和扫雷舰，为可能的扫雷行动做准备。

海上安全集团Dryad Global首席执行官科里·兰斯勒姆表示，即使在美国旨在摧毁伊朗军事能力（包括布雷船和库存）的打击之后，据估计伊朗仍拥有多达1000枚海军水雷。

“如果发现雷区，可能需要数周甚至数月才能消除威胁，”他说。

联合国航运机构负责人阿塞尼奥·多明格斯周一对重新开放霍尔木兹海峡的协议表示欢迎，称这是“恢复这条对海员和船舶至关重要的海上通道安全的的重要一步”。

“然而，其实施需要时间，以确保所有必要的安全和安保保障措施到位，”他说。

（报道：乔纳森·索尔和萨比娜·西博尔德；另有补充报道：米歇尔·罗斯、约翰·爱尔兰、蕾妮·马尔tezou、帕里萨·哈菲兹和安德鲁·米尔斯；编辑：雷切尔·阿姆斯特朗和艾丹·刘易斯）
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

LONDON, June 15 (Reuters) - Ensuring the Strait of Hormuz is safe from mines could delay a return to normal shipping traffic by weeks following a deal to reopen the waterway, shipping and maritime security sources say.

The operation by conventional minesweepers and state-of-the-art underwater drones could continue for 40 to 50 days before many insurance, shipping or oil companies are confident enough to sail through, according to assessments from five Western maritime security sources.

Advertisement

That could potentially hold up tens of millions of barrels of oil, in addition to the oil supply from the Gulf already blocked since the United States and Israel attacked Iran on February 28, according to estimates based on pre-war flows.

Every export barrel from the Gulf is crucial given stockpiles in the world’s largest economies are headed toward their lowest levels since at least 2003, according to analysis last week by the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

Even though Iran and the U.S. quietly helped ships pass through the blockaded waterway in recent weeks, shipping officials continued to urge caution after the U.S. and Iran said on Sunday they had reached a preliminary agreement to end their war and reopen the strait.

Advertisement

“We still consider it very risky for ships to commence transits at this point,” said Jakob Larsen, chief safety & security officer at shipping association BIMCO.

“The threat of mines in the area remains a concern immediately as well as further down the line and mine-free routes need to be established.”

Advertisement

Assurances Sought By Shippers

It is unclear how many mines Iran may have laid in the strait, which handled 20% of the world’s daily supply of oil and liquefied natural gas before the war.

Iran, which has sought to assert its control over the waterway during the war, has threatened to deploy naval mines, without commenting on whether its forces have planted them.

The U.S. has indicated that mines are a risk, and says it has targeted Iranian mine-laying boats.

On June 2, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio told a Senate Foreign Relations Committee hearing that Iran had “mined large segments of Hormuz — international waters”, without elaborating.

Advertisement

In a June 11 note, Germany’s navy, citing information from the U.S. and British navies, said mines were located in four locations around the strait, adding that the mine locations could not be verified by Germany.

Even the possibility of mines could deter companies. A supertanker and its cargo of crude are worth about $300 million, so war risk underwriters, oil and tanker companies would need assurances that passage is safe before they attempt to pass through the strait, shipping industry officials said.

Advertisement

“One sea mine is enough to have fatalities,” said Rene Kofod-Olsen, CEO of V.Group, one of the world’s top technical ship and crew management specialists which has 13 ships stuck in the Gulf.

“That’s obviously a massive issue for global shipping,” he said.

Shipping Traffic Remained Low

When asked last week about how many mines had been laid and locations, a spokesperson with the U.S. military’s Central Command (Centcom) said it was unable to publicly discuss specifics for operational security reasons.

“U.S. military efforts for ensuring the Strait of Hormuz is fully clear of sea mines laid by Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps are ongoing,” they said.

Advertisement

The White House did not respond to requests for comment.

Oman’s Maritime Security Centre warned mariners on May 30 navigating around its side of the strait to exercise caution after it described the sighting of an “object suspected to be a floating mine”.

Oman’s information ministry did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

As Iran and the U.S. negotiated over the interim deal to halt the war, both sides enabled some ships to exit the strait.

U.S. President Donald Trump said last week that the U.S. had been taking millions of barrels of oil out, and Reuters reported in May on how some states have struck deals with Tehran to secure passage for vessels.

Advertisement

The number of ships passing through the strait increased to an average of 12 to 15 vessels a day in recent weeks, according to shipping data that can be verified once the vessels become visible after leaving the strait. But that is a fraction of the 120 to 140 ships that passed through the waterway daily before the war.

Threat Of Mines

In March, before the agreement of a tenuous ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran, Iran’s National Defense Council said any attempt by “the enemy” to target Iranian coasts or islands would lead to mining of access routes and communication lines throughout the Gulf, according to Iran’s semi-official Fars news agency.

It said measures would include various types of naval mines, including floating mines that could be launched from shore.

Advertisement

Iran’s foreign ministry did not respond to a request for comment.

Britain, France and Germany have all despatched warships and minesweepers to the Middle East in anticipation of a potential mine clearing operation.

Corey Ranslem, chief executive of maritime security group Dryad Global, said even after U.S. strikes aimed at destroying Iranian military capacity including mine laying vessels and stocks, Iran was estimated to possess up to 1,000 naval mines.

“If there is a mine field detected, it could potentially take weeks or months to remove the threat,” he said.

Advertisement

Arsenio Dominguez, head of the U.N.’s shipping agency, on Monday welcomed the deal to reopen Hormuz as “an important step toward restoring safety in this vital maritime corridor for seafarers and ships”.

“However, its implementation will require time to ensure that all necessary safety and security guarantees are in place,” he said.

(Reporting by Jonathan Saul and Sabine Siebold, additional reporting by Michel Rose, John Irish, Renee Maltezou, Parisa Hafezi and Andrew Mills, editing by Rachel Armstrong and Aidan Lewis)

Advertisement
